next_steps: Biopsy of the affected orbital tissue/optic nerve sheath
Initiate systemic corticosteroids for symptomatic management
Evaluate serum IgG subclass profile and ANA/ANCA testing
Perform orbital/lacrimal gland biopsy if accessible
Consult rheumatology and ophthalmology for multidisciplinary management
Consider IgG4-RD immunohistochemical staining of prior or future biopsy specimens

differential_diagnoses: Systemic lupus erythematosus (specifically with ocular/orbital involvement)
next_steps: Perform serum testing for ANA, anti-dsDNA, anti-SSA/SSB, and ANCA
Re-evaluate prior abdominal skin biopsy with direct immunofluorescence (DIF) to rule out connective tissue disease
Obtain biopsy of new/active skin rash lesions for histopathology and DIF
Initiate empiric high-dose systemic corticosteroid therapy to prevent further visual deterioration
Perform orbital biopsy if the patient fails to respond to steroids or if diagnosis remains uncertain
Consult rheumatology to assist with management of suspected underlying autoimmune/inflammatory condition
